name: Dumbbell Bench Press
resistance_profile: Hardest in the deep stretch at the bottom, where the dumbbells drop below chest level for a longer range than a barbell; eases near lockout.
execution_steps: Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh, then lie back, using your legs to kick the weights up.
Press the dumbbells to arm's length over your chest with your shoulder blades pinched back and down.
Lower under control into a stretch, keeping elbows about 45 degrees from your torso.
Press the dumbbells up and slightly together, stopping just short of clashing them.
good_when: You want a deeper chest stretch and a more natural wrist and shoulder path than a barbell.
You train alone and want to bail safely by dropping the dumbbells.
You need to even out left-right strength differences.
bad_when: You want to lift maximal absolute load (a barbell lets you handle more).
Getting heavy dumbbells into the start position is your limiter.
